content: WebTransport lifecycle: TLS 1.3 handshake (1-RTT first connect, 0-RTT reconnect), then CONNECT stream establishes WebTransport session. Session closed when CONNECT stream closes. Three primitives: datagrams (unreliable, UDP-like), unidirectional streams (reliable, one-way), bidirectional streams (reliable, full-duplex). All multiplexed over one HTTP/3 connection with no head-of-line blocking between streams/datagrams. For signaling: use bidirectional streams (reliable delivery required for offer/answer/ICE). For potential relay of sensor data through server: use datagrams. SETTINGS_WT_MAX_SESSIONS must be >0 on both endpoints. wtransport Rust crate: accept() loop spawns tokio tasks per session; connection.accept_bi()/accept_uni() for streams; connection.receive_datagram()/send_datagram() for datagrams.
